column1: What is your current primary occupation?
column2: How often are you required to speak in public, lead meetings, or attend formal interviews?
column3: On a scale of 1 to 5, how much stress or anxiety do you typically experience before and during these events?
column4: During a high-stress presentation, which of the following physical or cognitive symptoms do you experience most frequently?
column5: How accurately do you feel you can perceive your own speaking pace and body language in real-time while under pressure?
column6: Have you ever received feedback (or watched a recording of yourself) and realized you performed differently than you felt at the time (e.g., spoke much faster or fidgeted without noticing)?
column7: If you were to use a digital tool to help you pace yourself during a speech, what would be your biggest concern?
column8: If a system could warn you that you are speaking too fast, which type of real-time feedback would you find the least distracting?
column9: How do you currently review or evaluate your presentations to improve for the future?
column10: Would you find it valuable to log into a Dashboard after your speech to review a "timeline" correlating your physiological stress (e.g., heart rate spikes) with your speaking pace?
